input.headline: One of the world’s most influential philanthropies is changing its name. Here’s why it matters.

raw.headline: One of the world’s most influential philanthropies is changing its name. Here’s why it matters.

winner: Why did the philanthropy change its name? It realized it had been *donating* to poverty for too long—it was time to *donate* to something that wouldn’t just keep coming back.

candidates.text: The Gates Foundation is rebranding as "The Stills Foundation"—because after all that giving away money, we're just sitting on what's left now.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on 'Gates Foundation' with a pun on 'stills,' cleverly tying it to wealth accumulation despite philanthropy."}

candidates.text: Why did the philanthropy change its name? It realized it had been *donating* to poverty for too long—it was time to *donate* to something that wouldn’t just keep coming back.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'donate,' but it's a bit generic and lacks specificity about the philanthropy or the name change. The humor is mild and relies more on puns than surprising insight."}

candidates.text: Why did the philanthropy change its name? Because it realized it was giving too much away and needed to keep more for itself!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 4.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat generic and relies on a common punchline about philanthropy being self-serving. It lacks specificity and surprise, and the humor feels forced in the context of a serious headline about a name change."}

candidates.text: We don't need another foundation—we already have one that just sits there and judges us.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat relevant and has a mild twist, but it lacks specificity and the surprise element is weak. The reference to 'judges us' is vague and doesn't clearly tie back to the headline about a philanthropy changing its name."}

candidates.text: I don’t get why they’re renaming philanthropy—it was already so generous!
